title: Asking About a Language Course
summary: A tourist walks into a Spanish language school in Buenos Aires and asks the director about course options, schedules, and prices for a two-week stay.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Good morning. I'd like to ask about your Spanish courses. I'm in Buenos Aires for two weeks.

body.speaker: Director
body.text: Welcome. We have several options. Could I ask your current level?

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: I'd say beginner. I know basic words like greetings, numbers, and a few useful phrases.

body.speaker: Director
body.text: Perfect. Our standard course is twenty hours a week, Monday to Friday, from nine in the morning to one in the afternoon.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Is that with other beginners only?

body.speaker: Director
body.text: Yes. Each class has between five and eight students at the same level. We also offer two private hours per week if you'd like more practice.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: How much does the standard course cost?

body.speaker: Director
body.text: For two weeks, it's two hundred and eighty dollars. The private hours are an extra forty dollars each.

body.speaker: Director
body.text: Yes, one textbook and a notebook. You'll also get access to our online practice site for one month.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: That sounds great. Do you organise any activities outside the school?

body.speaker: Director
body.text: We do — two cultural activities a week. Tango lessons on Tuesdays and a walking tour on Thursdays. Both are free for students.

body.speaker: Tourist
body.text: Excellent. Can I start next Monday?

body.speaker: Director
body.text: Of course. Please come at eight thirty for a short level test, and bring a passport-sized photo for your student card.

annotations.meaningVi: các khoá học tiếng Tây Ban Nha
annotations.exampleEn: Their Spanish courses start every Monday.
Please tell me your current level of English.
He knows basic greetings in five languages.
Most students choose the standard course.
She booked two private hours with her teacher.
There is an extra fee for the trip.
Open your textbook to page ten.

annotations.meaningVi: trang web luyện tập trực tuyến
annotations.exampleEn: Students get free access to the online practice site.
The school offers cultural activities every week.
Tango lessons are very popular here.
All students take a short level test on the first day.

grammarNotes.title: Polite question forms with 'Could' and 'Would'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'Could I…?' để xin phép, 'Would you like…?' để mời hoặc đưa ra lựa chọn. Trong bài: 'Could I ask your current level?' và 'Would you like more practice?'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'Could I…?' to ask permission politely, and 'Would you like…?' to offer something or check a preference. From the dialogue: 'Could I ask your current level?' and 'Would you like more practice?'

grammarNotes.title: Frequency phrases: 'twenty hours a week', 'two activities a week'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'số + đơn vị + a/per + đơn vị thời gian' diễn tả tần suất. Trong bài: 'twenty hours a week', 'two private hours per week', 'two cultural activities a week'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The pattern 'number + unit + a/per + time period' expresses frequency. From the dialogue: 'twenty hours a week', 'two private hours per week', 'two cultural activities a week'.

translationVi:
Tourist: Chào chị. Tôi muốn hỏi về các khoá tiếng Tây Ban Nha. Tôi ở Buenos Aires hai tuần.
Director: Chào mừng anh. Bên tôi có vài lựa chọn. Cho phép em hỏi trình độ hiện tại của anh được không?
Tourist: Chắc là beginner. Tôi biết vài từ cơ bản như lời chào, con số và mấy câu thông dụng.
Director: Tuyệt. Khoá tiêu chuẩn của trường là hai mươi tiết một tuần, thứ Hai đến thứ Sáu, từ chín giờ sáng đến một giờ chiều.
Tourist: Lớp chỉ có người mới bắt đầu thôi à?
Director: Vâng. Mỗi lớp có từ năm đến tám học viên cùng trình độ. Chúng tôi cũng có hai tiết học riêng mỗi tuần nếu anh muốn luyện nhiều hơn.
Tourist: Khoá tiêu chuẩn giá bao nhiêu ạ?
Director: Hai tuần là hai trăm tám mươi đô. Tiết riêng cộng thêm bốn mươi đô mỗi tiết.
Tourist: Có bao gồm sách không?
Director: Có ạ, một cuốn giáo trình và một cuốn vở. Anh còn được dùng trang luyện tập online của trường trong một tháng.
Tourist: Hay đấy. Trường có tổ chức hoạt động gì bên ngoài lớp không?
Director: Có ạ — hai hoạt động văn hoá mỗi tuần. Học khiêu vũ tango vào thứ Ba và đi bộ tham quan vào thứ Năm. Cả hai đều miễn phí cho học viên.
Tourist: Tuyệt vời. Tôi bắt đầu thứ Hai tới được không?
Director: Tất nhiên. Anh đến lúc tám rưỡi để làm bài kiểm tra trình độ ngắn, và mang theo một ảnh thẻ cho thẻ học viên nhé.

questions.prompt: How long will the tourist be in Buenos Aires?
questions.explanation: 'I'm in Buenos Aires for two weeks.'
questions.hint: Listen for the length of his stay.

questions.prompt: What are the standard course hours?
options: Twenty hours a week, Monday to Friday, 9:00–13:00
Fifteen hours a week, Tuesday to Saturday, 10:00–13:00
Twenty-five hours a week, every day
Ten hours a week in the evenings
questions.explanation: 'twenty hours a week, Monday to Friday, from nine in the morning to one in the afternoon.'
questions.hint: Listen for the total hours and the daily times.

questions.prompt: How many students are in each class?
questions.explanation: 'Each class has between five and eight students.'
questions.hint: Listen for a range of numbers.

questions.prompt: How many private hours can students add per week?
questions.explanation: 'We also offer two private hours per week.'

questions.prompt: How much does the standard course cost for two weeks?
questions.explanation: 'For two weeks, it's two hundred and eighty dollars.'

questions.prompt: How much does ONE private hour cost extra?
questions.explanation: 'The private hours are an extra forty dollars each.'

questions.prompt: What is included with the course materials?
options: A textbook, a notebook, and one month's online practice access
questions.explanation: 'one textbook and a notebook. You'll also get access to our online practice site for one month.'
questions.hint: Listen for two physical items and one digital item.

questions.prompt: What are the two free cultural activities each week?
options: Tango lessons on Tuesdays and a walking tour on Thursdays
A cooking class and a film night
questions.explanation: 'Tango lessons on Tuesdays and a walking tour on Thursdays.'
questions.hint: Listen for two activities with days.

questions.prompt: What time should the tourist arrive on Monday?
questions.explanation: 'Please come at eight thirty for a short level test.'
questions.hint: Listen for the arrival time, which is earlier than class.

questions.prompt: What should the tourist bring on the first day?
options: A passport-sized photo for his student card
questions.explanation: 'bring a passport-sized photo for your student card.'
questions.hint: Listen for what to bring for the student card.

cloze.template:
Tourist: Good morning. I'd like to ask about your Spanish {{b1}}. I'm in Buenos Aires for two weeks.
Director: Welcome. We have several options. Could I ask your current {{b2}}?
Tourist: I'd say beginner. I know basic words like greetings, numbers, and a few useful phrases.
Director: Perfect. Our standard course is twenty hours a {{b3}}, Monday to Friday, from nine in the morning to one in the afternoon.
Tourist: Is that with other beginners only?
Director: Yes. Each class has between five and eight {{b4}} at the same level. We also offer two {{b5}} hours per week if you'd like more practice.
Tourist: How much does the standard course cost?
Director: For two weeks, it's two hundred and eighty dollars. The private hours are an {{b6}} forty dollars each.
Tourist: Are books {{b7}}?
Director: Yes, one textbook and a notebook. You'll also get access to our online practice site for one month.
Tourist: That sounds great. Do you organise any activities outside the school?
Director: We do — two {{b8}} activities a week. Tango lessons on Tuesdays and a walking tour on Thursdays. Both are {{b9}} for students.
Tourist: Excellent. Can I start next Monday?
Director: Of course. Please come at eight thirty for a short level {{b10}}, and bring a passport-sized photo for your student card.

blanks.explanation: 'cultural' — relating to the culture of the place.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The school offers tango lessons and walking tours for language students. Do you think learning a language works better when it is mixed with culture and daily life, or when students stay focused only on grammar and vocabulary? Why?
